summary:
Twins with twin-twin transfusion syndrome (TTTS) are always genetically identical because the condition occurs solely in monochorionic pregnancies, which result from the splitting of a single zygote. This makes them monozygotic twins sharing complete genomic material derived from one fertilized egg.  

$\boxed{\text{A}}$
formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 2 summarized): Twins with twin-twin transfusion syndrome (TTTS) are always genetically identical because the condition occurs solely in monochorionic pregnancies, which result from the splitting of a single zygote. This makes them monozygotic twins sharing complete genomic material derived from one fertilized egg.  

$\boxed{\text{A}}$. Answer choice: A
